What was a 'privateer'?: A royal guard stationed at the naval ports to inspect incoming merchant cargo. They were essentially customs officers acting on behalf of the Queen.
A French mercenary hired to defend English shipping lines from Spanish raids. They were essentially paid soldiers acting on behalf of the Queen.
An explorer given a royal charter to establish a new colony in the New World. They were essentially royal governors acting on behalf of the Queen.
Who was England's most famous and succes: Sir Walter Raleigh. He was a famous courtier and explorer who drove the Virginia project forward.
Sir John Hawkins. He established the triangular trade route and designed new galleons.
Sir Richard Grenville. He was a naval commander who led the first group of colonists to Roanoke.
In what years did Sir Francis Drake comp: 1572–1575. He became the first Englishman to successfully sail completely around the globe.
1585–1588. He became the first Englishman to successfully sail completely around the globe.
1590–1593. He became the first Englishman to successfully sail completely around the globe.
Did Drake originally set out with the in: Yes. He had been given a secret royal charter by Elizabeth specifically to map the Pacific Ocean and find a northern shipping passage.
Yes. He had made a bet with other court investors that he could beat the Spanish record for the fastest global voyage.
No. He had been ordered to transport Dutch Protestant ambassadors to the Americas to establish a military alliance.
What was the name of Drake's only surviv: The Tiger. He set off with five ships, but only one survived the perilous three-year journey.
The Falcon. He set off with five ships, but only one survived the perilous three-year journey.
The Revenge. He set off with five ships, but only one survived the perilous three-year journey.
What territory in North America did Drak: Virginia (modern-day North Carolina). He was warmly welcomed by the local indigenous people and claimed the land for the English Crown.
New England (modern-day Massachusetts). He was warmly welcomed by the local indigenous people and claimed the land for the English Crown.
Florida (modern-day Miami). He was warmly welcomed by the local indigenous people and claimed the land for the English Crown.
Why was Drake's claim of Nova Albion hig: It resulted in Spain declaring an immediate trade embargo on all English cloth exports. The Spanish King viewed the claim as a hostile act.
It forced the French King to sign the Treaty of Joinville with Scotland. The French viewed the claim as a threat to their Atlantic trade.
It allowed Elizabeth to appoint Drake as the royal governor of the Americas. The Queen viewed the claim as the start of colonization.
Roughly how much stolen Spanish treasure: £50,000. This moderate haul of gold and silver was enough to pay off a small portion of the English national debt.
£100,000. This moderate haul of gold and silver was enough to pay off a small portion of the English national debt.
£1,000,000. This astonishing haul of gold and silver was enough to pay off the entire English national debt.
What percentage profit did Drake's priva: Around 100%. This moderate financial success inspired a massive boom in private investment for future English exploration.
Around 500%. This moderate financial success inspired a massive boom in private investment for future English exploration.
Around 1000%. This moderate financial success inspired a massive boom in private investment for future English exploration.
How did Elizabeth reward Drake upon his: She appointed him as the Lord High Admiral of the Navy. She did this to reward his maritime skill and secure her fleets.
She granted him vast estates in the North of England. She did this to reward his loyalty and increase his noble status.
She named him as her chief Secretary of State. She did this to reward his intelligence and replace William Cecil.
Why was Drake's knighting highly signifi: It forced Philip II to declare an immediate trade embargo on all English merchants. The Spanish King viewed the knighting as a direct insult.
It led to the Pope issuing the excommunication bull against Elizabeth. The Pope viewed the knighting as an endorsement of piracy.
It convinced France to sign a defensive alliance with England against Spain. The French viewed the knighting as a show of naval strength.
How did the circumnavigation impact Engl: It proved England was a major global military power capable of launching a land invasion of Spain's colonies in Europe.
It proved England was a major global trade partner capable of supplying cloth directly to the markets in Asia and America.
It proved England was a major global religious leader capable of spreading the Reformation to the native people in the New World.
Who was the royal favourite given the ch: Robert Dudley. He was a famous courtier and explorer who drove the Virginia project forward.
Francis Walsingham. He was a famous courtier and explorer who drove the Virginia project forward.
Thomas Howard. He was a famous courtier and explorer who drove the Virginia project forward.
Did Raleigh personally lead the colonist: Yes. He commanded the flagship Tiger during the dangerous crossing and founded the settlement on Roanoke Island himself.
Yes. He spent three years living in the colony, managing the trade, and negotiating with the local Native American chiefs.
No. He was captured by Spanish ships during the crossing and held as a prisoner in Florida, organizing the colony from his cell.
What was Raleigh's main role in the Virg: He commanded the naval fleet that defended the colonists from Spanish attacks in the Caribbean during the crossing.
He acted as the governor of the colony, enforcing laws and managing the food supplies on Roanoke Island.
He negotiated with the Native American chiefs to buy land and secure food supplies for the incoming settlers.
What strategic military reason did Engla: To launch a land invasion of Spanish Florida. This would allow England to capture the Spanish gold mines and control the Gulf of Mexico.
To build a naval base to blockade the Spanish coast. This would prevent the Spanish Armada from sailing towards the English Channel.
To train Protestant militias in colonial warfare. This would provide a reserve army that could be brought back to defend England.
What economic commodities did the Englis: Spices, silk, tea, and porcelain. They hoped to barter English cloth for these valuable goods.
Wheat, barley, timber, and iron. They hoped to barter English cloth for these valuable goods.
Cotton, coffee, silver, and copper. They hoped to barter English cloth for these valuable goods.
Why was establishing trade in Virginia p: It would lower land rents in England by encouraging emigration. By reducing poverty, it would prevent peasant rebellions.
It would provide a source of gold to back the English currency. By increasing the money supply, it would lower inflation.
It would make the merchant class richer than the traditional nobility. By shifting power, it would secure the Queen's government.
Who were Manteo and Wanchese?: Two English sailors who had lived with the Algonquian tribe for a year. They acted as translators and guides for the colonists.
Two French explorers who had mapped the coast of Virginia. They sold their maps and journals to Raleigh to fund the voyage.
Two Catholic priests who had run away from Spanish Florida. They converted to Protestantism and helped the English establish the colony.
How did Thomas Harriot contribute to the: He mapped the coast of Roanoke Island and built the defensive fort. This helped the colonists secure their settlement.
He raised the private investment funds from London merchants. This helped Raleigh finance the ships and purchase supplies.
He acted as the doctor for the expedition, researching native plants. This helped the colonists find medicine and survive diseases.
How did Raleigh use Manteo and Wanchese: He had them baptized in a public Protestant ceremony in London. He used them as proof that they could convert the native people.
He trained them to display English farming methods in public parks. He used them as proof that the land was fertile.
He had them speak to Parliament about the gold mines in Virginia. He used them as proof that the colony would be highly profitable.
In what year did the first group of 107: 1584. The expedition was led by the naval commander Richard Grenville.
1586. The expedition was led by the naval commander Richard Grenville.
1587. The expedition was led by the naval commander Richard Grenville.
Why was the timing of their departure a: They left England during a major outbreak of the plague. This meant that many colonists were already sick when they arrived.
They left England during the winter storms, which delayed their arrival. This meant they ran out of food during the crossing.
They left England at the same time the Spanish Armada was sailing. This meant they were immediately attacked by Spanish ships.
What vital supplies were ruined when sea: The maps, navigational instruments, and books describing the territory. This disaster left the captains completely lost.
The gifts, cloth, and iron goods intended for trade with the natives. This disaster made it impossible to buy food.
The bricks, tools, and timber required to build the houses. This disaster left the colonists without any shelter.
What was the main flaw in the 'mix of pe: They were mostly merchants and bankers seeking trade contracts. They critically lacked the military skills needed to defend the fort.
They were mostly women and young children seeking a new life. They critically lacked the physical strength needed to clear the forest.
They were mostly Puritans seeking religious freedom. They critically lacked the willingness to cooperate with the royal officers.
Who was Ralph Lane?: The commander of the naval fleet. He was a brilliant sailor whose sea experience ensured the safe crossing of the Atlantic.
The chief scientist and mapmaker. He was an educated scholar who created the first dictionary of the Algonquian language.
The Native American chief of the Roanoke tribe. He was a friendly leader who initially welcomed and fed the English colonists.
Why did the local Algonquian chief, Wing: He discovered that the English were secret allies of the Spanish in Florida. He feared they would help Spain conquer his tribe.
He objected to the colonists trying to convert his people to Protestantism. He feared this would destroy their traditional religion.
He was angered by the English refusing to help his tribe fight their traditional enemies. He viewed them as useless allies.
What ultimately happened to Chief Wingin: He died of a European disease brought by the colonists. This caused the tribe to believe the English had cast a lethal spell on them.
He escaped to the mainland and raised a massive tribal army to besiege the fort. This forced the colonists to abandon the settlement.
He signed a peace treaty with Governor Ralph Lane, agreeing to supply the colony. This treaty was later broken by his successor.
What major European conflict prevented s: The French Civil War. Elizabeth ordered all English ships to remain at home to defend against potential French raids in the Channel.
The Dutch Revolt. Elizabeth ordered all English ships to remain at home to transport troops and supplies to the Netherlands.
The Spanish Inquisition in Flanders. Elizabeth ordered all English ships to remain at home to protect Protestant merchants.
How did the surviving 1585 colonists fin: They built a temporary wooden ship and sailed across the Atlantic themselves, arriving half-starved in Cornwall in 1586.
They surrendered to a Spanish patrol fleet from Florida and were transported back to Europe as prisoners of war in 1586.
They walked north through the forests to a French settlement in Canada, hitching a ride home on a French merchant ship in 1586.
